name: Cyan, Inc. v. Beaver County Employees Retirement Fund
summary: The Court held that the term 'subsection' in a statutory cross-reference means what it says and should not be read to refer to something narrower.
significance: Cited by amicus for the principle that when Congress says 'subsection,' it means 'subsection' — supporting the argument that (b)(3)(C)'s reference to 'this subsection' encompasses all of subsection (b), including (b)(1).
